title: Weldaloy — Client Gifting at Scale
summary: 300 custom solar chargers to every client — then 500 premium cable kits shipped to France. They come back for quality.
card_teaser: Aerospace manufacturer gifting programs with custom packaging and global delivery.
client: Weldaloy — aerospace parts manufacturer, metro Detroit. Norm F., Marketing Director.
goal: Deliver high-quality branded gifts to their client base — gifts that reflect Weldaloy's standards and make recipients feel valued.
challenge: Client gifts needed to feel premium, not promotional — custom presentation mattered as much as the product.
Scale: hundreds of units, each with consistent branding and packaging.
Follow-on program raised the bar — metal-housing chargers, decorated case, international delivery.
solution: Program 1: 300 solar-powered battery chargers — custom box decorated inside and out, custom product placeholder, delivered to Weldaloy's clients.
Program 2 (~May 2026): 500 high-quality charger cables (iPhone + USB-C) in metal housing; unit and case both decorated; shipped to France for a partner/client.
Curated product selection focused on quality over commodity — Weldaloy chose us when they wanted it done right.
result: Recipients loved the first program — Norm reached out in January: "Can we get any more? Great feedback — people loved the gifts."
Follow-on order in May: "I got great feedback from those. Do we have any more?" — 500 premium cable kits, shipped to France.
Weldaloy treats us as their quality partner for client gifting — not a catalog vendor.
takeaway: When client gifting has to impress — and scale — curated products plus custom packaging and reliable delivery earn repeat programs.

quote.text: Can we get any more? Great feedback — people loved the gifts.
quote.name: Norm F.
quote.title: Marketing Director, Weldaloy

quote_followup.text: I got great feedback from those. Do we have any more?
quote_followup.name: Norm F.
quote_followup.title: Marketing Director, Weldaloy

title: Awecomm AI Conference — Holiday Gifting Made Easy
summary: Holiday gifting program launch at a Detroit-area tech conference — 20+ decision-maker conversations and immediate orders.
card_teaser: Curated holiday bundles and conversation-first booth for consulting and tech clients.
client: Awecomm (event partnership) — technology and consulting professionals, ~150–200 attendees.
goal: Introduce Holiday Gifting Made Easy — reposition from embroidery shop to brand experience partner that helps companies give gifts people are proud to present.
challenge: Short preparation time to design, produce, and align all materials.
Stand out among technology and consulting professionals.
Intimate audience of existing clients and partners — trust and value, not hard sales.
New gifting offer needed a clear message that connected personally and professionally.
solution: Custom microsite with curated holiday bundles, checkout, and follow-up automation.
Elegant booth (Rose Gold + Deep Blue) with stress-free gifting messaging.
Yeti cooler giveaway ($500 value) reinforcing product quality.
Conversation-first approach — helping attendees envision curated year-end gifts.
result: 20+ meaningful conversations with decision-makers.
Immediate orders after the event and ongoing follow-ups with multiple companies.
Strengthened relationships with Awecomm leadership and clients.
Reinforced positioning as strategic gifting partner, not a commodity vendor.
takeaway: Branded experiences connect physical gifts with emotional impact — turning giveaways into thoughtful moments that strengthen business relationships.

title: Ven Johnson Law — Nats at the Zoo
summary: 800+ hoodies, tees, and hats for Kalamazoo's Nats at the Zoo — sold out. Ongoing work with Marketing Director Holly Q.
card_teaser: Premium event apparel and marketing projects for a major Michigan law firm.
client: Ven Johnson Law — major Michigan law firm. Holly Q., Marketing Director (marketing and creative lead).
goal: Premium apparel and branded items for Nats at the Zoo — an annual July tennis tournament in Kalamazoo — plus ongoing marketing projects that reflect the firm's standards.
challenge: Event apparel had to move fast and feel premium — not generic law-firm promo.
High volume (~400 hoodies, ~400 t-shirts, plus additional shirts and hats) with tight event timing.
Marketing team needed one partner for varied projects — event apparel, executive gifts, sponsored athlete gear — not a new vendor every time.
solution: Premium hoodies, t-shirts, and hats at scale for Nats at the Zoo — Holly updated throughout production.
Executive pens and other branded items for firm marketing.
Custom jersey for a college tennis player the firm sponsors.
Additional projects as needs arise — one point of contact with Holly.
result: Event apparel moved faster than any prior year — items ran out at the tournament.
Ongoing relationship across events, apparel, and marketing projects.
Holly Q. as direct partner on creative and production — trusted for quality and responsiveness.
takeaway: When a marketing director needs event apparel that actually gets taken, plus pens, jerseys, and hats from the same partner — consistency and quality build a long-term creative relationship.

title: Karmanos — Signed Boxing Gloves for Cancer Survivors
summary: 25 custom embroidered gloves — staff-signed and gifted to notable cancer survivors. Now on their fundraiser committee.
card_teaser: When embroidery seemed impossible and the gift had to mean something.
client: Karmanos — nonprofit / cancer care. Program year: 2024.
goal: Create a one-of-a-kind gift worthy of Karmanos's mission — originally envisioned for major donors, ultimately directed to cancer survivors the hospital identified as notable.
challenge: Gloves with foam interiors made embroidery nearly impossible for most vendors. The gift also had to carry real emotional weight — not feel like promotional product.
solution: Deconstructed the cuff, embroidered, and reassembled for a professional, durable finish. ~25 high-end gloves produced. Karmanos staff signed each glove personally.
result: Gloves gifted to cancer survivors selected by the hospital — a deeply personal recognition moment.
Ongoing trust relationship: invited to join a 2025 fundraiser committee.
Continued introductions and connections — Karmanos treats us as a partner, not a vendor.
takeaway: Some gifts aren't about logo placement — they're about solving the impossible and helping an organization honor people who matter.
note: Original brief referenced Tommy Hearns signing; final program used staff signatures for survivor recipients.

title: Orfin & Associates — Gifting Catalog & Client Appreciation
summary: 80 blankets in 10 days when 4imprint couldn't — now a curated gifting catalog and custom holiday client gifts.
card_teaser: Insurance & financial — from emergency save to "you're my guy."
client: Orfin & Associates — insurance and financial. Laura D., primary contact.
goal: Reliable partner for monthly client appreciation and invite events — plus curated client gifting programs that are easy to reorder.
challenge: First call: ~80 blankets needed with only 10 days until the event. 4imprint and big-box vendors couldn't deliver.
Ongoing need: monthly events, tight deadlines, personal service online vendors can't provide.
Holiday client gifts needed to feel premium — not generic promo.
solution: Delivered 80 blankets on an impossible timeline — first proof of partnership.
Built a curated gifting collection catalog customized for Orfin — easy browse, easy reorder.
Holiday party client gift: fresh buttery shortbread drop cookies (baked days before delivery) in a fully customized box with lid and silver tie.
Ongoing: apparel, koozies, pens, ribbon-cutting scissors, event items — one point of contact.
result: Laura D.: "You're my guy. You make it really easy for me. Unless you don't offer it, I'm not going anywhere else."
Gifting catalog live — orders placed as needed without starting from scratch each time.
Holiday shortbread gift was a hit with clients at their party.
Refers us to others when they hear of needs in other industries.
takeaway: We turn "is this even possible?" into a long-term gifting partnership — deadline saves earn trust that catalogs never will.

title: Northstar Painting — Durable Long-Sleeve Work Tees
summary: Consultative apparel upgrade — crews looked professional longer on the job.
card_teaser: Solving worn-out cuffs with better product research and free samples.
client: Northstar Painting.
goal: Provide durable long-sleeve tees for daily work use.
challenge: Their old shirts had cuffs that wore out quickly, making employees look sloppy and unprofessional.
solution: Researched alternatives, provided free test samples, and switched them to a Hanes product built for durability.
result: The new shirts held up longer, looked better on the job, and eliminated employee complaints. The owner felt confident his crews represented the business well.
takeaway: We consult to solve hidden problems that directly affect a business's professional image.

title: Thornton & Grooms — Webstore, Jackets & Client Gifting Pipeline
summary: ~$20K year one, custom jacket stores for all staff — now in talks for annual gifts to 10,000 subscription clients.
card_teaser: From chaotic multi-vendor ordering to program partner — and expanding into client gifting.
client: Thornton & Grooms — one of Michigan's largest HVAC and plumbing companies. Karen B. (webstore), Candice H. (Marketing), Katrina J. (original contact).
goal: One reliable partner for all branded apparel and promotional needs — simple, predictable ordering for management and staff.
challenge: Quotes didn't match final invoices — sometimes by large amounts.
Staff ordering from cheap online sites; hidden costs and confusing checkout.
No clear owner when issues arose across multiple vendors.
solution: Custom webstore live ~1.5 years — transparent live pricing, one point of contact.
Centralized apparel, jackets, beanies, and promo gear.
Dedicated jacket stores so every employee could try samples for size and color before ordering — repeated annually and expanding.
Karen B. used as reference — helped win another company webstore.
result: ~$20,000 in year one; program still growing.
Karen B.: "You're the best. You make my job so easy — I feel like I don't have to do anything."
Candice H.: "I'd love to get you some more business in this company. We love working with you."
In active talks: annual client gifting for ~10,000 HVAC/plumbing subscription customers — curated catalog built, first gift set in planning.
takeaway: Turnkey merch programs earn trust first — then expand into client gifting at scale. Thornton & Grooms went from vendor chaos to treating us like staff.

title: Averna — Precision DNA Embroidery
summary: Fine-detail DNA helix embroidery when another vendor said "this is the best we can do."
card_teaser: North Face jackets with 1-inch DNA helix detail — CEO-approved.
client: Averna — medical research company.
goal: Embroidered North Face jackets reflecting the company's precision and premium image.
challenge: Logo included extremely fine details, including a 1-inch DNA helix. A previous vendor failed to execute.
solution: Adjusted stitch density, used thinner thread and specialized needles, ran multiple test samples, and fine-tuned machine tension.
result: Client called the work "perfect." Even the highly particular CEO was satisfied. They continue ordering and have referred others.
takeaway: Precision embroidery that matches the highest client standards — even when others say it can't be done.

goal: Supply and embroider lab coats for hundreds of medical staff every month.
challenge: Execution required a partner who could handle 300–600 lab coats monthly with strict consistency.
solution: Managed sourcing, embroidery, and supply of all lab coats with professional finishes at scale.
result: Delivered thousands of coats reliably for years, proving capability for massive recurring orders.
takeaway: Systems and reliability to manage hundreds of items every month with consistent branding.

result: Member loved the work and referred us to the club as a trusted resource.
